evals.expected_output: Full cost report with project breakdown, top sessions, model breakdown, daily spend chart, token cost breakdown, model recommendations, and MCP usage summary if applicable.
expectations: Report includes a 'PROJECT BREAKDOWN' section header
PROJECT BREAKDOWN table has columns: Project, Sessions, Cost
Report includes a 'MODEL BREAKDOWN' section header
MODEL BREAKDOWN table has a 'Cost' column
Report includes a 'TOKEN COST BREAKDOWN' section header
Report includes a 'DAILY SPEND' section header
Report includes a 'MODEL RECOMMENDATIONS' section header
Header shows pricing source — either 'platform.claude.com (live)' or 'hardcoded fallback'
Grand total cost in header is a positive dollar amount (e.g., '$X.XX')
Header includes 'Sessions:' count as a positive integer
If no sessions used MCP tools, 'MCP USAGE DETECTED' section is absent

evals.expected_output: Same report as full analysis but filtered to last 7 days. Period in header should reflect a 7-day window.
expectations: Header period spans 7 days or fewer (period_end - period_start <= 7 days)
Header shows exact period start and end dates in YYYY-MM-DD format
Report includes 'PROJECT BREAKDOWN' section
Report includes 'MODEL BREAKDOWN' section
Report includes 'DAILY SPEND' section
No sessions in the report data have dates older than 7 days from today
If run history exists with matching filters, a 'vs last run' comparison line appears in the header

evals.expected_output: Report filtered to last 30 days, showing only top 5 most expensive sessions in the TOP SESSIONS table. All other sections (project breakdown, model breakdown, etc.) still show full 30-day aggregates.
expectations: TOP SESSIONS table contains at most 5 data rows (excluding header and separator lines)
Header period spans 30 days or fewer
PROJECT BREAKDOWN still shows all projects from the 30-day period (not top-5 limited)
Grand total in header reflects all 30-day sessions, not just the top 5
MODEL BREAKDOWN reflects all 30-day sessions

evals.expected_output: MCP-focused report. Standard project breakdown and model breakdown are kept, but Top Sessions / Daily Spend / Token Cost Breakdown / Model Recommendations are replaced by MCP sections: Server Configuration, Tool Usage Breakdown, Context Overhead Analysis, MCP Sessions Detail, and Optimization Recommendations.
expectations: Report includes 'MCP SERVER CONFIGURATION' section header
MCP SERVER CONFIGURATION section lists configured servers with their type and usage status
Report includes 'MCP TOOL USAGE BREAKDOWN' section header
Report includes 'CONTEXT OVERHEAD ANALYSIS' section header
Report includes 'MCP OPTIMIZATION RECOMMENDATIONS' section header
Report does NOT include 'MODEL RECOMMENDATIONS' section
Report does NOT include 'DAILY SPEND' section
Report still includes 'PROJECT BREAKDOWN' section

evals.expected_output: Report filtered to sessions between March 1 and March 15, 2026. Header shows this exact date range.
expectations: Header period start is on or after 2026-03-01
Header period end is on or before 2026-03-15
No sessions in the report have dates before 2026-03-01
No sessions in the report have dates after 2026-03-15
If zero sessions matched the date range, report states 'No sessions matched the given filters'

evals.expected_output: Full cost report displayed in chat, then saved to ~/test-report.md. Claude confirms the save with the file path.
expectations: Report is displayed in chat as normal before save confirmation
Claude confirms save with a message containing the file path (e.g., 'Report saved to ~/test-report.md')
File ~/test-report.md exists after run and contains the 'PROJECT BREAKDOWN' section
Saved file contains the full report including header, project breakdown, and model breakdown

evals.expected_output: Full cost report with an additional budget status line in the header showing monthly pace vs the $500 budget.
expectations: Header contains a 'Budget:' line showing the budget amount ($500/month)
Header contains a 'Pace:' metric showing the computed monthly spending pace
Header contains a 'Status:' field showing either 'OVER' or 'UNDER' with percentage difference
All standard sections (PROJECT BREAKDOWN, MODEL BREAKDOWN, etc.) are still present
Budget status line appears directly in the header block, not as a separate section

evals.expected_output: When 200+ sessions exist and no date filter is given, a warning appears before the report suggesting --days to reduce scope. Standard report sections follow.
expectations: If session count exceeds 200 and no --days/--since/--until flag was used, a warning message appears before the report body
Warning mentions the session count and suggests using '--days 30' or similar
Standard 'PROJECT BREAKDOWN' section still appears after the warning
Standard 'MODEL BREAKDOWN' section still appears
Warning does not prevent the report from being generated
